kids.question: Who wrote the letter to the Ephesians?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 1:1 — "Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to the saints which are at Ephesus."

kids.question: What has God blessed us with according to Ephesians 1?
kids.option_a: All spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ
kids.explanation: Ephesians 1:3 — "who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ."

kids.question: Where is Jesus sitting right now according to Paul?
kids.option_a: At God's right hand in heaven
kids.explanation: Ephesians 1:20 — "set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places."

teens.question: When did God choose us according to Ephesians 1:4?
teens.option_a: Before the foundation of the world
teens.option_b: At the moment of our baptism
teens.explanation: Ephesians 1:4 — "he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world."

teens.question: Through what do we have redemption?
teens.option_a: Through His blood, the forgiveness of sins
teens.explanation: Ephesians 1:7 — "In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins."

teens.question: What does Paul call the Holy Spirit in relation to our inheritance?
teens.option_a: The earnest (guarantee/deposit) of our inheritance
teens.explanation: Ephesians 1:14 — "Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession."

teens.question: What does Paul pray the Ephesians would receive?
teens.option_a: The spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Him
teens.explanation: Ephesians 1:17 — "the God of our Lord Jesus Christ… may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him."

teens.question: What is the church called in relation to Christ?
teens.option_a: His body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all
teens.explanation: Ephesians 1:22-23 — "gave him to be the head over all things to the church, Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all."

adults.question: What does Paul say about God's predestination in verse 5?
adults.option_a: He predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ, according to the good pleasure of His will
adults.option_b: He predestinated only the apostles
adults.option_c: Predestination is based on foreseen merit
adults.option_d: He predestinated the fall of mankind
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:5 — "Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will."

adults.question: What is the "mystery of His will" Paul mentions?
adults.option_a: To gather together in one all things in Christ, both in heaven and earth
adults.option_b: The date of Christ's return
adults.option_d: The location of the lost tribes
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:9-10 — "the mystery of his will… That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one all things in Christ."

adults.question: What two things does Paul say believers were sealed with?
adults.option_a: The Holy Spirit of promise, after hearing the word of truth (the gospel of salvation)
adults.option_b: Water baptism and circumcision
adults.option_c: The blood of the Passover lamb and anointing oil
adults.option_d: The sign of the cross and chrismation
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:13 — "after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise."

adults.question: What three things does Paul pray the Ephesians would know (eyes of understanding enlightened)?
adults.option_a: The hope of His calling, the riches of His glorious inheritance in the saints, and the exceeding greatness of His power toward believers
adults.option_b: Past sins, present duties, and future rewards
adults.option_c: The law, the prophets, and the writings
adults.option_d: Their spiritual gifts, their calling, and their church role
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:18-19 — "the hope of his calling… the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints, And what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe."

adults.question: What does Paul say Christ's exalted position is far above?
adults.option_a: All principality, power, might, dominion, and every name that is named in this world and the world to come
adults.option_b: Only earthly kings and rulers
adults.option_d: Only the Roman emperor
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:21 — "Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come."

adults.question: What power does Paul compare to God's power working in believers?
adults.option_a: The same mighty power that raised Christ from the dead and seated Him at God's right hand
adults.option_b: The power that parted the Red Sea
adults.option_c: The power that created the world in six days
adults.option_d: The power that held back the flood
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:19-20 — "according to the working of his mighty power, Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead."

adults.question: What does "to the praise of the glory of his grace" repeated in this chapter emphasize?
adults.option_a: That God's ultimate purpose in election, redemption, and sealing is His own glory — the refrain appears in verses 6, 12, and 14
adults.option_b: That believers should praise loudly in worship
adults.option_c: That only grace matters, not holiness
adults.option_d: That God seeks human applause
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:6,12,14 — The phrase "to the praise of his glory" appears three times, emphasizing God's glory as the ultimate purpose.

adults.question: How does Paul describe God's working in redemption in verse 8?
adults.option_a: He abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence
adults.option_b: He acted in strict justice without mercy
adults.option_c: He worked through natural human reasoning
adults.option_d: He revealed everything at once without progression
adults.explanation: Ephesians 1:8 — "Wherein he hath abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence."

kids.question: What does Paul say we are saved by?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 2:8 — "For by grace are ye saved through faith."

kids.question: What does Paul say we are — God's what?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 2:10 — "we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works."

kids.question: Who is our peace, making Jews and Gentiles one?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 2:14 — "he is our peace, who hath made both one."

teens.question: What were believers before God made them alive?
teens.option_a: Dead in trespasses and sins
teens.explanation: Ephesians 2:1 — "And you hath he quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins."

teens.question: What is salvation NOT of, according to Ephesians 2:8-9?
teens.option_a: Not of yourselves and not of works, lest any man should boast
teens.explanation: Ephesians 2:8-9 — "not of yourselves: it is the gift of God: Not of works, lest any man should boast."

teens.question: What did Christ break down between Jews and Gentiles?
teens.option_a: The middle wall of partition
teens.explanation: Ephesians 2:14 — "hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us."

teens.question: What does Paul say Gentile believers are no longer?
teens.option_a: Strangers and foreigners, but fellowcitizens with the saints and of the household of God
teens.explanation: Ephesians 2:19 — "ye are no more strangers and foreigners, but fellowcitizens with the saints, and of the household of God."

teens.question: What is the church built upon according to verse 20?
teens.option_a: The foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ being the chief corner stone
teens.option_c: The foundation of the law of Moses
teens.explanation: Ephesians 2:20 — "built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone."

adults.question: What three influences controlled believers before conversion according to verses 2-3?
adults.option_a: The course of this world, the prince of the power of the air, and the lusts of the flesh and mind
adults.option_b: Poverty, ignorance, and disease
adults.option_c: Greek philosophy, Roman law, and Jewish tradition
adults.option_d: Fear, pride, and laziness
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:2-3 — "the course of this world… the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that now worketh in the children of disobedience… the lusts of our flesh… the desires of the flesh and of the mind."

adults.question: What motivated God to save us according to verse 4?
adults.option_a: His great love and His being rich in mercy
adults.option_b: Our good works impressed Him
adults.option_d: The angels interceded for us
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:4 — "God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us."

adults.question: What three things has God done for believers "together with Christ" in verses 5-6?
adults.option_a: Quickened us together, raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places
adults.option_b: Forgiven us, baptized us, and confirmed us
adults.option_c: Taught us, disciplined us, and rewarded us
adults.option_d: Called us, chosen us, and glorified us
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:5-6 — "hath quickened us together with Christ… hath raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus."

adults.question: What is God's purpose in the ages to come according to verse 7?
adults.option_a: To show the exceeding riches of His grace in His kindness toward us through Christ Jesus
adults.option_b: To establish believers as earthly rulers
adults.option_c: To demonstrate His wrath against sin
adults.option_d: To reveal hidden mysteries to angels
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:7 — "That in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through Christ Jesus."

adults.question: What does "created in Christ Jesus unto good works" imply about the role of works?
adults.option_a: Good works are the result and purpose of salvation, not its cause — they were prepared beforehand for believers to walk in
adults.option_b: Works are required before salvation
adults.option_c: Works are optional for the saved
adults.option_d: Works earn additional salvation
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:10 — "created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them."

adults.question: What does Paul say Christ abolished in His flesh?
adults.option_a: The enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances, to make of two one new man
adults.option_b: The Roman legal system
adults.option_d: The priesthood of Aaron
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:15 — "Having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances; for to make in himself of twain one new man, so making peace."

adults.question: How does Paul describe believers' access to God in verse 18?
adults.option_a: Through Christ we both (Jews and Gentiles) have access by one Spirit unto the Father
adults.option_b: Only through a human priest
adults.option_d: Through our own merits
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:18 — "For through him we both have access by one Spirit unto the Father."

adults.question: What is the church growing into according to verse 21?
adults.option_a: A holy temple in the Lord, a habitation of God through the Spirit
adults.option_b: A political force in the Roman Empire
adults.option_d: A replacement for the Jerusalem temple
adults.explanation: Ephesians 2:21-22 — "groweth unto an holy temple in the Lord: In whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of God through the Spirit."

kids.question: What mystery did God reveal to Paul?
kids.option_a: That Gentiles are fellow heirs with Jews in Christ
kids.explanation: Ephesians 3:6 — "the Gentiles should be fellowheirs, and of the same body."

kids.question: What does Paul say God can do beyond what we ask?
kids.option_a: Exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think
kids.explanation: Ephesians 3:20 — "able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think."

kids.question: What did Paul pray that Christ would do in the Ephesians' hearts?
kids.option_a: Dwell in their hearts by faith
kids.explanation: Ephesians 3:17 — "That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith."

teens.question: What does Paul call himself in relation to the Gentiles?
teens.option_a: The prisoner of Jesus Christ for you Gentiles
teens.option_d: The teacher of the Gentiles only
teens.explanation: Ephesians 3:1 — "I Paul, the prisoner of Jesus Christ for you Gentiles."

teens.question: What does Paul call himself — the least of what?
teens.option_a: Less than the least of all saints
teens.explanation: Ephesians 3:8 — "Unto me, who am less than the least of all saints, is this grace given."

teens.question: What four dimensions of Christ's love does Paul pray they would know?
teens.option_a: The breadth, length, depth, and height
teens.explanation: Ephesians 3:18 — "May be able to comprehend with all saints what is the breadth, and length, and depth, and height."

teens.question: What did Paul say the mystery of Christ was hidden from in past ages?
teens.option_a: Other ages — it was not made known to the sons of men as now revealed to apostles and prophets by the Spirit
teens.explanation: Ephesians 3:5 — "Which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men, as it is now revealed unto his holy apostles and prophets by the Spirit."

teens.question: What does Paul ask the Ephesians not to lose heart about?
teens.option_a: His tribulations (sufferings) for them, which are their glory
teens.option_b: The slow growth of the church
teens.option_d: The cost of building a meeting place
teens.explanation: Ephesians 3:13 — "I desire that ye faint not at my tribulations for you, which is your glory."

adults.question: What is the "dispensation of the grace of God" given to Paul?
adults.option_a: The stewardship of proclaiming the mystery that Gentiles are fellow heirs, of the same body, and partakers of the promise in Christ by the gospel
adults.option_b: A special healing ministry
adults.option_c: Authority to write all the epistles
adults.option_d: The role of high priest for the church
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:2,6 — "the dispensation of the grace of God which is given me to you-ward… the Gentiles should be fellowheirs, and of the same body."

adults.question: To whom is the manifold wisdom of God made known through the church?
adults.option_a: Principalities and powers in heavenly places
adults.option_b: The Roman senate
adults.option_d: Future generations of humanity
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:10 — "To the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly places might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of God."

adults.question: What does Paul say about boldness and access to God in verse 12?
adults.option_a: In Christ we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of Him
adults.option_b: We must approach God with fear and trembling only
adults.option_c: Only apostles have direct access to God
adults.option_d: Access requires priestly mediation
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:12 — "In whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him."

adults.question: What does Paul pray the Father would grant according to the riches of His glory?
adults.option_a: To be strengthened with might by His Spirit in the inner man
adults.option_b: To be blessed with material wealth
adults.option_d: To be made physically strong
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:16 — "he would grant you, according to the riches of his glory, to be strengthened with might by his Spirit in the inner man."

adults.question: What does Paul say Christ's love surpasses?
adults.option_a: Knowledge — it passeth knowledge, that they might be filled with all the fulness of God
adults.option_b: Human experience only
adults.option_d: The love of angels
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:19 — "to know the love of Christ, which passeth knowledge, that ye might be filled with all the fulness of God."

adults.question: According to what power does God work in believers?
adults.option_a: According to the power that worketh in us — the same power referenced in Ephesians 1:19-20
adults.option_b: According to our faith level
adults.option_d: According to our knowledge
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:20 — "according to the power that worketh in us."

adults.question: What does Paul's doxology in 3:20-21 ascribe glory to?
adults.option_a: God in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, world without end
adults.option_b: God in the temple at Jerusalem
adults.option_d: God in heaven alone, not on earth
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:21 — "Unto him be glory in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, world without end. Amen."

adults.question: How does Paul describe his role as a minister of the gospel?
adults.option_a: He was made a minister according to the gift of the grace of God given by the effectual working of His power
adults.option_b: He chose the ministry as a career
adults.option_c: He inherited the role from his family
adults.option_d: He was appointed by the Jerusalem council
adults.explanation: Ephesians 3:7 — "Whereof I was made a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God given unto me by the effectual working of his power."

kids.question: How many Lord, faith, and baptism does Paul say there are?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 4:5 — "One Lord, one faith, one baptism."

kids.question: What does Paul say we should speak to each other?
What should we do instead of stealing?
kids.option_a: Work with our hands so we can give to those in need
kids.option_c: Ask someone else to steal for us
kids.explanation: Ephesians 4:28 — "let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth."

teens.question: What does Paul urge believers to walk worthy of?
teens.option_a: The vocation wherewith they are called, with lowliness, meekness, longsuffering
teens.explanation: Ephesians 4:1 — "walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called."

teens.question: What five ministry gifts did Christ give to the church?
teens.option_a: Apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, and teachers
teens.option_b: Deacons, elders, bishops, priests, and monks
teens.option_c: Healers, miracle workers, speakers, singers, and writers
teens.option_d: Kings, priests, prophets, judges, and warriors
teens.explanation: Ephesians 4:11 — "he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers."

teens.question: What does Paul say about not letting the sun go down on?
teens.option_a: Your wrath (anger)
teens.explanation: Ephesians 4:26 — "let not the sun go down upon your wrath."

teens.question: What should believers put off and put on?
teens.option_a: Put off the old man (corrupt according to deceitful lusts) and put on the new man (created in righteousness and true holiness)
teens.option_b: Put off old clothes and put on new ones
teens.option_c: Put off sadness and put on happiness
teens.option_d: Put off work and put on rest
teens.explanation: Ephesians 4:22-24 — "put off concerning the former conversation the old man… put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness."

teens.question: Who does Paul say believers should not grieve?
teens.option_a: The Holy Spirit of God, by whom they are sealed unto the day of redemption
teens.explanation: Ephesians 4:30 — "grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption."

adults.question: What seven unities does Paul list in verses 4-6?
adults.option_a: One body, one Spirit, one hope, one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God and Father
adults.option_b: Seven churches, seven seals, seven trumpets
adults.option_c: Seven gifts, seven virtues, seven blessings
adults.option_d: One temple, one priesthood, one sacrifice, one altar, one feast, one law, one covenant
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:4-6 — "One body, and one Spirit… one hope… One Lord, one faith, one baptism, One God and Father of all."

adults.question: What Psalm does Paul quote about Christ's ascension?
adults.option_a: "When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men" (Psalm 68:18)
adults.option_b: "The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand" (Psalm 110:1)
adults.option_c: "Lift up your heads, O ye gates" (Psalm 24:7)
adults.option_d: "The stone which the builders refused is become the head stone" (Psalm 118:22)
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:8 — "When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men."

adults.question: What is the purpose of the five ministry gifts?
adults.option_a: For the perfecting of the saints, the work of the ministry, and the edifying of the body of Christ
adults.option_b: To govern the church politically
adults.option_d: To collect tithes and manage finances
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:12 — "For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ."

adults.question: What does Paul say about Christ descending before ascending?
adults.option_a: He first descended into the lower parts of the earth before ascending far above all heavens, that He might fill all things
adults.option_b: He descended from a mountain into a valley
adults.option_c: He went down to the river for baptism
adults.option_d: He descended from heaven only once at the incarnation
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:9-10 — "he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth… He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things."

adults.question: What does Paul say about Gentile believers' former manner of thinking?
adults.option_a: They walked in the vanity of their mind, having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through ignorance and blindness of heart
adults.option_b: They were wise philosophers seeking truth
adults.option_c: They were simple but sincere seekers
adults.option_d: They were morally superior to Jewish unbelievers
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:17-18 — "walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind, Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God."

adults.question: What does "being past feeling" describe about unregenerate Gentiles?
adults.option_a: They gave themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness — their conscience was seared
adults.option_b: They could not feel physical pain
adults.option_d: They had no capacity for joy
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:19 — "Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness."

adults.question: What does Paul say about corrupt communication?
adults.option_a: "Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers"
adults.option_b: "Only speak in tongues when in church"
adults.option_d: "Use only Hebrew in worship"
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:29 — "Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying."

adults.question: What six things does Paul say to put away, and what should replace them?
adults.option_a: Bitterness, wrath, anger, clamour, evil speaking, malice — replaced by kindness, tenderheartedness, and forgiveness as God forgave us in Christ
adults.option_b: Poverty, sickness, ignorance, weakness, loneliness, fear
adults.option_c: Fasting, prayer, giving, preaching, teaching, serving
adults.option_d: Wine, meat, luxury, idleness, travel, entertainment
adults.explanation: Ephesians 4:31-32 — "Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another."

kids.question: What does Paul say children of light should walk in?
kids.option_a: Love, as Christ also hath loved us
kids.explanation: Ephesians 5:2 — "walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us."

kids.question: What does Paul say to find out?
kids.option_a: What is acceptable unto the Lord
kids.explanation: Ephesians 5:10 — "Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord."

kids.question: What should we be filled with instead of wine?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 5:18 — "be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit."

teens.question: What does Paul say Christ gave Himself as?
teens.option_a: An offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour
teens.explanation: Ephesians 5:2 — "Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour."

teens.question: What should have no fellowship with?
teens.option_a: The unfruitful works of darkness; rather reprove (expose) them
teens.explanation: Ephesians 5:11 — "have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them."

teens.question: How does Paul say we should walk?
teens.option_a: Circumspectly (carefully), not as fools but as wise, redeeming the time
teens.explanation: Ephesians 5:15-16 — "See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise, Redeeming the time, because the days are evil."

teens.question: What relationship does Paul compare to Christ and the church?
teens.option_a: Husband and wife
teens.explanation: Ephesians 5:25 — "Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church."

teens.question: How did Christ love the church?
teens.option_a: He gave Himself for it, to sanctify and cleanse it
teens.option_b: He ruled over it with strict laws
teens.option_d: He left it to manage itself
teens.explanation: Ephesians 5:25-26 — "Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it; That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the fruit of the Spirit (or Light)?
adults.option_a: The fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth
adults.option_b: The fruit of the Spirit is in tongues, healing, and prophecy
adults.option_c: The fruit of the Spirit is only for apostles
adults.option_d: The fruit of the Spirit replaces moral living
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:9 — "the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth."

adults.question: What does Paul quote that says "Awake thou that sleepest"?
adults.option_a: "Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light" — likely an early Christian hymn or prophetic adaptation of Isaiah 60:1
adults.option_b: "Arise, shine; for thy light is come" (Isaiah 60:1) exactly as written
adults.option_c: A quote from the book of Enoch
adults.option_d: A saying of Jesus from the Gospels
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:14 — "Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light."

adults.question: What does Paul say about understanding the will of the Lord versus drunkenness?
adults.option_a: "Be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit" — contrasting fleshly excess with spiritual fullness
adults.option_b: Wine is forbidden for all Christians
adults.option_d: Only priests should abstain from wine
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:17-18 — "Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord is. And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit."

adults.question: What does being filled with the Spirit produce according to verses 19-21?
adults.option_a: Speaking in psalms, hymns, spiritual songs; giving thanks always; submitting to one another in the fear of God
adults.option_b: Only speaking in tongues
adults.option_d: Withdrawal from all social interaction
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:19-21 — "Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord; Giving thanks always… Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God."

adults.question: How does Paul describe Christ's goal for the church?
adults.option_a: To present it to Himself a glorious church, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but holy and without blemish
adults.option_b: To make it the largest organization on earth
adults.option_c: To give it political power over nations
adults.option_d: To make it wealthy and influential
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:27 — "That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish."

adults.question: What Old Testament text does Paul quote about marriage?
adults.option_a: "For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh" (Genesis 2:24)
adults.option_b: "It is not good that the man should be alone" (Genesis 2:18)
adults.option_d: "Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing" (Proverbs 18:22)
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:31 — "For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh."

adults.question: What does Paul call the marriage-Christ/church parallel?
adults.option_a: "This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church"
adults.option_b: "This is a simple illustration"
adults.option_d: "This is a cultural accommodation"
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:32 — "This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church."

adults.question: What does Paul say the wife's submission is compared to?
adults.option_a: As the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing
adults.option_b: As slaves are to masters
adults.option_d: As soldiers are to generals
adults.explanation: Ephesians 5:24 — "as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing."

kids.question: What does Paul tell children to do?
kids.option_a: Obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right
kids.explanation: Ephesians 6:1 — "Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right."

kids.question: What armor does Paul say to put on?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 6:11 — "Put on the whole armour of God."

kids.question: What is the sword of the Spirit?
kids.explanation: Ephesians 6:17 — "the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God."

teens.question: What is the first commandment with a promise?
teens.option_a: "Honour thy father and mother; that it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth"
teens.explanation: Ephesians 6:2-3 — "Honour thy father and mother; which is the first commandment with promise."

teens.question: What does Paul say fathers should not do to their children?
teens.option_a: Provoke them to wrath, but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord
teens.option_c: Let them do whatever they want
teens.explanation: Ephesians 6:4 — "ye fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord."

teens.question: Who is our real enemy according to Ephesians 6?
teens.option_a: Principalities, powers, rulers of darkness, and spiritual wickedness in high places
teens.explanation: Ephesians 6:12 — "we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places."

teens.question: Name the six pieces of the armor of God.
teens.option_a: Belt of truth, breastplate of righteousness, shoes of the gospel of peace, shield of faith, helmet of salvation, sword of the Spirit
teens.option_b: Sword, spear, shield, helmet, armor, and horse
teens.option_c: Prayer, fasting, giving, reading, singing, and serving
teens.option_d: Faith, hope, love, patience, kindness, and goodness
teens.explanation: Ephesians 6:14-17 lists the belt of truth, breastplate of righteousness, shoes of the gospel of peace, shield of faith, helmet of salvation, and sword of the Spirit.

teens.question: What does the shield of faith quench?
teens.option_a: All the fiery darts of the wicked
teens.explanation: Ephesians 6:16 — "the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked."

adults.question: How does Paul instruct servants (bondservants) to work?
adults.option_a: With fear and trembling, in singleness of heart as unto Christ, not with eyeservice as menpleasers, doing the will of God from the heart
adults.option_b: Only when the master is watching
adults.option_d: With the goal of earning their freedom
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:5-6 — "Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters… with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ; Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart."

adults.question: What does Paul say masters should do, and why?
adults.option_a: Do the same things (treat servants well), forbearing threatening, knowing that their Master is in heaven and there is no respect of persons with Him
adults.option_b: Rule with an iron fist
adults.option_d: Pay them wages equal to free men
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:9 — "ye masters, do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your Master also is in heaven; neither is there respect of persons with him."

adults.question: Why does Paul say to put on the whole armor of God?
adults.option_a: To be able to stand against the wiles (schemes) of the devil
adults.option_b: To impress other churches
adults.option_d: To prove our salvation
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:11 — "Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil."

adults.question: What does the phrase "having done all, to stand" mean in context?
adults.option_a: After using every piece of armor and fighting the spiritual battle, the believer remains standing firm — perseverance in spiritual warfare
adults.option_b: After finishing your daily chores, stand still
adults.option_c: After preaching a sermon, stand at the door
adults.option_d: After communion, remain standing
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:13 — "having done all, to stand."

adults.question: What kind of prayer does Paul call for in spiritual warfare?
adults.option_a: "Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints"
adults.option_b: A specific prayer said once daily
adults.option_d: Prayer only during church services
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:18 — "Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints."

adults.question: What does Paul ask the Ephesians to pray for regarding his ministry?
adults.option_a: That utterance may be given to him to make known with boldness the mystery of the gospel, for which he is an ambassador in bonds
adults.option_b: That he be released from prison
adults.option_d: That he be given safety in travel
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:19-20 — "that utterance may be given unto me, that I may open my mouth boldly, to make known the mystery of the gospel, For which I am an ambassador in bonds."

adults.question: Who does Paul send to the Ephesians and for what purpose?
adults.option_a: Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister, to inform them of Paul's affairs and comfort their hearts
adults.option_b: Timothy, to be their new pastor
adults.option_d: Apollos, to teach them eloquence
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:21-22 — "Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, shall make known to you all things… that he might comfort your hearts."

adults.question: What unique phrase does Paul use in his closing benediction?
adults.option_a: "Grace be with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity (incorruption)"
adults.option_b: "The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit"
adults.option_c: "May the peace of God guard your hearts"
adults.option_d: "Greet one another with a holy kiss"
adults.explanation: Ephesians 6:24 — "Grace be with all them that love our Lord Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen."
